title: Multi-horizon stop-imminence targets instead of only next-lap binary
summary: Recent F1 pit-stop papers report gains from architectures that jointly predict next-stop classification and stop timing/horizon, implying the signal is smoother across nearby laps than a single binary cut.
rationale: Recent F1 pit-stop papers report gains from architectures that jointly predict next-stop classification and stop timing/horizon, implying the signal is smoother across nearby laps than a single binary cut. For this task, predicting pit-within-{1,2,3,5} laps or distance-to-next-stop as auxiliary labels should regularize the noisy decision boundary around actual stop laps.
implementation_hint: Create leakage-safe labels within each driver-race trajectory: binary targets for stop within H laps and optionally clipped distance-to-next-stop. Train CatBoost/XGB multitask-style via separate models and feed their OOF outputs into the final next-lap model or a tiny meta-learner; enforce monotone consistency across horizons at blend time.
expected_effect: Usually improves recall on borderline laps and yields decorrelated OOF signals versus the current pure next-lap classifier.
risk: Overlapping horizon labels can inflate CV if folds are not purged around event boundaries; auxiliary targets may collapse to the same ranking if engineered naively.
